active.name: Trinket
active.effects: Consume a charge to place a visible {{tip|Effigy}} at the target location, which grants {{tip|sight}} over the surrounding area for {{pp|130 + (300-130)/17*(x-1) for 20}} seconds. For enemies, it visually appears identical to {{ci|Fiddlesticks}} (including on the minimap) and has no visible [[health bar]] until it ''activates''. Enemy {{tip|champion|champions}} that approach it will activate it, causing it to automatically sound a Danger [[ping]] to its allies as well as fake a random action for up to 2 seconds. If not destroyed by that time, it will deal 1 damage to itself.

effects_structured.raw: Consume a charge to place an Effigy that grants vision for 130 to 300 seconds (scales with level).
Effigy appears as Fiddlesticks to enemies and has no visible health bar until activation.
Enemy champions that approach activate the effigy, causing a danger ping and fake action for up to 2 seconds.
If not destroyed after activation, the effigy self-destructs (deals 1 damage to itself).

schema_notes.effects_structured: Parsed from passive/active text for simulation use.
context_notes: Execution model: Scarecrow Effigy is a charge-based trinket placement with vector-targeted placement range and level-scaled lifetime vision payload.
Player-visible behavior: enemy approach activates disguise behavior, triggers danger ping/fake-action animation for up to 2 seconds, then self-destructs if not destroyed first.

Page notes include additional edge rules (hard-crowd-control death behavior, untargetability window, and item-haste cooldown scaling) that are documented but not fully encoded as structured combat-state rules.
Wave 120 (2026-02-25): `on_activate` entries with no fixed cooldown now use explicit `activation_cadence` charge-consumption schema.
